title: Intelligence Assessment
summary: Using intelligence to inform strategic decisions.
details: Good intelligence enables good decisions. Assess: enemy fleet composition, positions, capabilities, intentions, morale. Evaluate source reliability. Update assessments continuously. Intelligence gaps are themselves information - unknown enemy positions require caution. Counter-intelligence: assume enemy knows what you broadcast. Deception can create false intelligence for enemy. Act on best available information, not perfect information.
annotations: Decisions made on bad intelligence are bad decisions.
Perfect intelligence is rare - decide anyway.
